data.title: Compass Diversified announces $100M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Compass Diversified (NYSE:CODI) announced that its Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to $100M of CODI’s issued and outstanding common shares.
  • Under the authorization, CODI may purchase common shares through December 31, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Palo Alto Networks snaps six straight session of gains
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 0.35% lower to $373.13 on Wednesday.

The U.S.-based cybersecurity company gained over 5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has gained about 25% so far this year, compared to the nearly 22% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

According to Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, Palo Alto Networks has a Hold rating with a score of 3.25 out of 5. The company received an A+ for profitability, but it got an F in valuation.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and consider the stock a Hold.

However, 41 Wall Street analysts gave PANW a Buy and above. 13 have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Strong Sell.

Seeking Alpha Analyst Richard Durant said Palo Alto's cash flows are a highlight for the company, but they don't really reflect the profitability of the business.

"Palo Alto's business will continue to perform reasonably well, driven by its NGS portfolio, but I see little reason to own the stock at such a high valuation," he added.

data.title: Benitec started at outperform by Oppenheimer on gene therapy potential
data.description:
• Oppenheimer started coverage of Benitec Biopharma (NASDAQ:BNTC) at outperform, citing its gene therapy candidate BB-301 for the treatment of ocular pharyngeal muscular dystrophy, or OPMD.
  • The investment bank said it sees worldwide sales of around $438M for BB-301 in 2030, as it would likely be the first therapy approved for the condition.
  • It added that it sees the product priced at around $3M, in line with rare disease gene therapies such as Elevidys and Beqvez.
  • Oppenheimer said continued positive results in Phase 1/2 testing could allow for a pivotal study in 2026, with possible approval in 2028.
  • “We see BNTC (BNTC) flying under the radar compared to other gene therapies that are trading around $400M market cap and see significant upside potential over the next 12-18 months,” Oppenheimer added.
  • Oppenheimer set its price target for the stock at $35.

data.title: Moody's lowers outlook for Southwest Airlines as strategy initiatives present risks
data.description:
Southwest Airlines’ (NYSE:LUV) strong balance sheet and its solid position in the U.S. domestic market earned a Baa1 senior unsecured debt rating at Moody’s, but the execution risk in Southwest’s strategy to improve operating margins and achieve $1B of free cash flow by 2027 prompted the ratings agency to lower its outlook to negative from stable.

To head off a proxy fight with Elliott Management, Southwest (LUV) revealed several initiatives to boost demand, increase free cash flow, and maximize the utilization of its aircraft. Moody’s warns, however, that if these measures aren’t successful and if performance in 2025 and 2026 falls short of the carrier’s expectations, free cash flow would fall below the $2B that Moody’s projects is needed for these years.

Additionally, Moody’s expects incremental improvement to Southwest’s operating margin to around 7.0% in 2026, but to achieve 10% operating margin and strong free cash flow less the anticipated proceeds from aircraft sales will take three or more years.

“Liquidity will remain strong notwithstanding the negative free cash flow,” Moody’s says, projecting cash plus short-term investments of near $5B at the end of 2025 and near $3.5B at the end of 2026.

Moody’s warns that a downgrade could occur if Southwest (LUV) doesn’t realize the expected returns on its strategy to improve profitability; if debt/EBITDA approaches 3x; if retained cash flow to net debt is sustained below 20% by the end of 2025; or, if liquidity falls below $5B.

data.title: Chord Energy, California Resources rated new Buys at UBS
data.description:
Chord Energy (NASDAQ:CHRD) +1.6% and California Resources (NYSE:CRC) +1.7% in Wednesday's trading as UBS initiates coverage of both names at Buy with respective $168 and $68 price targets, while starting Antero Resources (AR) and Civitas Resources (CIVI) at Neutral.

UBS says its positive outlook on Chord (CHRD) is supported by improving operational efficiency as the company integrates Enerplus, a strong balance sheet supporting 75% of free cash flow being returned to shareholders, and an attractive valuation that does not reflect its strong fundamentals.

The bank views California Resources (CRC) as a "unique E&P, with a diversified asset base that differentiates it vs. peers," including power, real estate and an emerging carbon capture and storage business, with upside from the CCUS unit valued at $14/share compared to $4 currently implied in the stock.

UBS sees balance sheet leverage as a key risk for Antero Resources (AR), which is unhedged next year, and while Civitas (CIVI) boasts the highest FY 2025 free cash flow yield in its coverage, significant debt reduction over the next six quarters may be difficult in a lower WTI environment.

data.title: The U.S. budget deficit: Where it's at today
data.description:
The United States has run an annual budget deficit for the 23rd straight year -- a period that runs through both Democratic and Republican-led governments, and that offers a perennial worry to budget-hawk investors.

The U.S. Treasury Department has yet to release final numbers for September (the last month of the fiscal year). However, the chart above shows a breakdown of the mounting 2024 deficit by month. The only monthly surplus came in April, when "tax day" prompted a surge in revenue.

For 2024, receipts for the fiscal year increased by 11% (about $479 billion), to $4.918 trillion, while outlays rose an estimated 10% (about $617 billion) to $6.135 trillion.

Chart shows only complete fiscal years. Source: U.S. Treasury

Over the past 50 years, the country's books have resulted in a year-end surplus just 4 times -- most recently a small surplus in 2001. While the U.S. budget is on a streak of 23 straight deficits, the largest of those have come alongside some exogenous shocks: A four-year streak of bigger deficits following the Global Financial Crisis, and a few more following the worldwide supply shocks of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Drilling down into the state of the deficit this year: How does it add up (or in this case, fall short)? Here's a chart of YTD deficit spending (not including September), illustrating receipts and outlays to get a visual idea of the relative proportions:

Source: U.S. Treasury

Readers interested in investing topics tied to the upcoming election can find more information at the upcoming Seeking Alpha Investing Forum: Election 2024; please visit the event website.

data.title: Antofagasta keeps full-year copper production guidance after Q3 jumps
data.description:
Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF) +4% in Wednesday's trading after reporting a 15% jump in Q3 copper production compared to the prior quarter, while maintaining previous guidance for full-year production and cash costs.

The Chilean miner said copper production rose to 179K metric tons in the quarter from 155,300 tons in Q2 and up 3.4% from 173,600 tons in the year-earlier quarter, driven by a partial destocking of inventories at Los Pelambres and better copper grades and recoveries at Centinela.

Q3 gold production surged 54% Q/Q to 51,800 oz from 33,600 oz in Q2, reflecting higher gold grades at Centinela concentrates, but fell 9.5% Y/Y from 57,400 oz.

Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF) maintained its FY 2024 guidance for production at the lower end of the 670K-710K metric tons and cash costs both before and after by-product credits at $2.40/lb and $1.70/lb respectively; capital spending guidance for the full year was unchanged at $2.7B.

The company's reiteration of full-year guidance suggests expectations for a strong Q4, Citi analysts say, as "the implied Q4 copper production works out to ~200K tons, reflecting a strong ask for the sequential increase and if delivered would be a strong finish to the year."

data.title: Hurricane Milton seen costing insurers as much as $50B - report
data.description:
Damage from Hurricane Milton is expected to cause insured losses of $30B-$50B, Insurance Journal reported Wednesday, citing an estimate from catastrophe modeler Verisk.

The range includes privately insured losses to onshore property due to wind, storm surge, and precipitation-induced flood.

Milton struck Florida's Tampa Bay area last week as a Category 3 storm, arriving less than two weeks after Hurricane Helene made landfall in the U.S. and caused significant destruction inland.

"In addition to claims settlement challenges, the back-to-back coastal flooding events could lead to aggregate flood limits being reached in some cases,” Verisk said, as quoted by Insurance Journal.

Verisk's estimate aligns with others released by risk modeling firms since the hurricane, the article said. Karen Clark & Co., for example, expects Milton to cost insurers about $36B.

P&C insurers: American International Group (AIG), Aflac (AFL), Trisura Group (OTCPK:TRRSF), Travelers Companies (TRV), Chubb (CB), Allstate (ALL), Hartford Financial Services Group (HIG), Aon (AON), Progressive (PGR), Marsh & McLennan Companies (MMC).
